lines.english: There are many ways of being awake, and sleeping.
Naam Dayv and Jai Dayv are awake in this Dark Age of Kali Yuga. ||2||
Shiva is awake, serving at the Lord's Feet.
Hanuman with his tail is awake and aware.
says Kabeer, is to meditate and vibrate on the Lord's Name. ||3||2||
To be awake as Gurmukh is the most excellent way.
The most sublime of all the actions of this body,
The wife gives birth to her husband.
Without breasts, the mother nurses her baby. ||1||
The son leads his father in play.
Behold, people! This is how it is in the Dark Age of Kali Yuga.
Without feet, the mortal jumps.
The son marries his mother. ||1||Pause||
Without a mouth, he bursts into laughter.
Without travelling, a long journey is made.
Without udders, the cow gives milk.
Without feeling sleepy, he lays down and sleeps.
Without a churn, the milk is churned. ||2||
Says Kabeer, see this, and understand. ||3||3||
Without the True Guru, the path is not found.
Prahlaad was sent to school.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੀ ਪਟੀਆ ਲਿਖਿ ਦੇਹੁ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੋੁਪਾਲ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: meree patteea likh dehu sree goupaal |1|
lines.english: Write the Name of the Dear Lord on my tablet." ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋ ਕਉ ਕਹਾ ਪੜੑਾਵਸਿ ਆਲ ਜਾਲ ॥
lines.translit: mo kau kahaa parraavas aal jaal |
lines.english: He asked his teacher, "Why do you teach me about worldly affairs?
He took many of his friends along with him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਹੀ ਛੋਡਉ ਰੇ ਬਾਬਾ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ॥
lines.translit: nahee chhoddau re baabaa raam naam |
lines.english: O Baba, I will not forsake the Name of the Lord.
Sanda and Marka went to the king to complain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੋ ਅਉਰ ਪੜੑਨ ਸਿਉ ਨਹੀ ਕਾਮੁ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: mero aaur parran siau nahee kaam |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I will not bother with any other lessons. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਝੁ ਤੁਰਤੁ ਛਡਾਊ ਮੇਰੋ ਕਹਿਓ ਮਾਨਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: tujh turat chhaddaaoo mero kahio maan |2|
lines.english: I shall release you at once, if you obey my words." ||2||
He sent for Prahlaad to come at once.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਰਾਮ ਕਹਨ ਕੀ ਛੋਡੁ ਬਾਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: too raam kehan kee chhodd baan |
lines.english: He said to him, "Stop uttering the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਰਾਮੁ ਨ ਛੋਡਉ ਗੁਰਹਿ ਗਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: eik raam na chhoddau gureh gaar |
lines.english: I shall not forsake the One Lord; if I did, I would be going against my Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭਿ ਜਲ ਥਲ ਗਿਰਿ ਕੀਏ ਪਹਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: prabh jal thal gir kee pahaar |
lines.english: God created the water, land, hills and mountains.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋ ਕਉ ਕਹਾ ਸਤਾਵਹੁ ਬਾਰ ਬਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: mo kau kahaa sataavahu baar baar |
lines.english: Prahlaad answered, "Why do you annoy me, over and over again?

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋ ਕਉ ਘਾਲਿ ਜਾਰਿ ਭਾਵੈ ਮਾਰਿ ਡਾਰਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: mo kau ghaal jaar bhaavai maar ddaar |3|
lines.english: You might as well throw me into the fire and kill me." ||3||
The king became angry and drew his sword.
"Show me your protector now!"
He killed Harnaakhash, tearing him apart with his nails. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਥੰਭ ਤੇ ਨਿਕਸੇ ਕੈ ਬਿਸਥਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: prabh thanbh te nikase kai bisathaar |
lines.english: So God emerged out of the pillar, and assumed a mighty form.
The Supreme Lord God, the Divinity of the divine,
for the sake of His devotee, assumed the form of the man-lion.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਬੀਰ ਕੋ ਲਖੈ ਨ ਪਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: keh kabeer ko lakhai na paar |
lines.english: Says Kabeer, no one can know the Lord's limits.
He saves His devotees like Prahlaad over and over again. ||5||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਗਿਆਨ ਰਤਨੁ ਹਿਰਿ ਲੀਨ ਮੋਰ ॥
lines.translit: jin giaan ratan hir leen mor |
lines.english: which has stolen my jewel of spiritual wisdom.
I am a poor orphan, O God; unto whom should I complain?

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਸੁ ਤਨ ਮਨ ਮਧੇ ਮਦਨ ਚੋਰ ॥
lines.translit: eis tan man madhe madan chor |
lines.english: Within the body and mind are thieves like sexual desire,

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੋ ਕੋ ਨ ਬਿਗੂਤੋ ਮੈ ਕੋ ਆਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: ko ko na bigooto mai ko aaeh |1|
lines.english: Who has not been ruined by sexual desire? What am I? ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੋ ਚਪਲ ਬੁਧਿ ਸਿਉ ਕਹਾ ਬਸਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: mero chapal budh siau kahaa basaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: What power does my fickle mind have against it? ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਧਉ ਦਾਰੁਨ ਦੁਖੁ ਸਹਿਓ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: maadhau daarun dukh sahio na jaae |
lines.english: O Lord, I cannot endure this agonizing pain.
The poets and the Yogis with their matted hair
Sanak, Sanandan, Shiva and Suk Dayv
were born out of Brahma's naval chakra.
all lived their lives with good behavior. ||2||
You are Unfathomable; I cannot know Your depth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਦੀਨਾ ਨਾਥ ਦੁਖੁ ਕਹਉ ਕਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: prabh deenaa naath dukh khau kaeh |
lines.english: O God, Master of the meek, unto whom should I tell my pains?
There is one merchant and five traders.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਰੋ ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਦੁਖੁ ਆਥਿ ਧੀਰ ॥
lines.translit: moro janam maran dukh aath dheer |
lines.english: Please rid me of the pains of birth and death, and bless me with peace.
Kabeer utters the Glorious Praises of God, the Ocean of peace. ||3||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹਿ ਐਸੇ ਬਨਜ ਸਿਉ ਨਹੀਨ ਕਾਜੁ ॥
lines.translit: mohi aise banaj siau naheen kaaj |
lines.english: I don't care at all about such commerce.
There are nine poles which hold the ten bags.
The body is tied by the seventy-two ropes. ||1||
The twenty-five oxen carry false merchandise.
